mission.title: kyverno: [Bug] [CLI] CRD Support for new Policy Types without Cluster connection
mission.description: [Bug] [CLI] CRD Support for new Policy Types without Cluster connection. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify kyverno troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kyverno deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno -n kyverno --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kyverno configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
Currently it is not possible to use the new Policy Types (ValidatingPolicy, ImageValidatingPolicy, MutatingPolicy, etc...) against a CRD if no cluster connection (with the required CRD installed) is available.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug] [CLI] CRD Support for new Policy Types without…
steps.description:
Currently it is not possible to use the new Policy Types (ValidatingPolicy, ImageValidatingPolicy, MutatingPolicy, etc...) against a CRD if no cluster connection (with the required CRD installed) is available. So, this PR enables support for applying these new policy types against CRDs even in the absence of a cluster connection. It allows users to work with CRDs locally, making it possible to validate or mutate resources defined by CRDs using Kyverno CLI or in offline environments.
- Enabled

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm [Bug] [CLI] CRD Support for new Policy Types… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno -n kyverno --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Currently it is not possible to use the new Policy Types (ValidatingPolicy, ImageValidatingPolicy, MutatingPolicy, etc...) against a CRD if no cluster connection (with the required CRD installed) is available. So, this PR enables support for applying these new policy types against CRDs even in the absence of a cluster connection.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kyverno installed or the issue environment reproducible.
